intro: Hieracium fariniramum, also known as Meadow Hawkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium fariniramum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiproliferative activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Hieracium fariniramum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
